violations.text: Food contact surfaces of deli slicer and vegetable slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Veggie slicer was placed in 3-bay sink. Deli slicer was taken apart and in process of being sanitized. Slicer must be broken down and sanitized after every use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

All foods were observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Walk-in unit was adjusted to 40 degrees. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025
An accumulation of old droppings were observed in the corner of the dry storage area. Area to be cleaned and monitored. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. PIC passed food safety course about 1 week ago and is waiting for certification number to submit CFSM paperwork. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

All foods in walk-in were observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC adjusted walk-in at the time of inspection. Ambient dropped to 39. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pasta was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered plastic container. Container was uncovered at the time of inspection. Cooling methods handout provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

The floor behind equipment in the back kitchen area has an accumulation of droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

Water used to poach eggs were observed stored under the handwashing sink in the kitchen. Water is stored in an area susceptible to splash and cross-contamination. Contamination must be prevented during storage. Water was relocated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023
A container was observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service during time of inspection and must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Corned beef hash was observed held at 94 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, corned beef hash had been left out of refrigeration for about an hour. Corned beef hash was placed on a sheet pan and relocated to the walk-in cooler to cool down during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

French Toast mix was observed held at 60 degrees Fahrenheit on the cart in the kitchen. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, French toast mix was out of refrigeration for about an hour. French toast mix was relocated to the refrigerator during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Sausage patties and linguica were observed held at 51 & 54 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep units. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Both were observed stored over the fill line. Per person in charge, both were placed in the prep unit about an hour prior to inspection. Sausage patties and linguica were both relocated under the fill line to cool during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Cheese was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Employees' medicines were not stored to prevent the contamination of food, equipment, or utensils. Medication was observed stored on the counter in the kitchen during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Leafy greens were observed stored under the handwashing sink in the kitchen. Leafy greens are stored in an area susceptible to splash and cross-contamination. Contamination must be prevented during storage. Leafy greens were relocated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023
